---
title: "eta_squared"
generated: true
description: "Eta-squared similarity between two profiles (Cohen et al., 2008)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.graph.parcellation`](../nitrix-graph-parcellation.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:eta_squared -->
```python
eta_squared(x: Float[Array, '... d'], y: Float[Array, '... d']) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:eta_squared -->
Eta-squared similarity between two profiles (Cohen et al., 2008).

Computes $\eta^2 = 1 - \mathrm{SS}_{\mathrm{within}} /
\mathrm{SS}_{\mathrm{total}}$ where, over the profile axis,
$\mathrm{SS}_{\mathrm{within}} = \sum_i (x_i - m_i)^2 + (y_i -
m_i)^2$ with $m_i = (x_i + y_i) / 2$ the per-element pair mean, and
$\mathrm{SS}_{\mathrm{total}} = \sum_i (x_i - M)^2 + (y_i - M)^2$
with $M$ the grand mean of both profiles.  The result ranges over
$[0, 1]$ (attaining $1$ iff `x == y`); it is the canonical
functional-connectivity profile-similarity measure, more robust than the
Pearson correlation to a global offset or scale between profiles, and is
differentiable.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:eta_squared -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `x` | `Float` | `...` × `d` | — | Profiles with the comparison along the last axis; leading axes broadcast. |
| `y` | `Float` | `...` × `d` | — | Profiles with the comparison along the last axis; leading axes broadcast. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`. Eta-squared similarity, with the broadcast leading shape of `x` and `y` (the comparison axis reduced away).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:eta_squared -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
